sections.paragraph:
ἐκπλήσσω (attic -ττω) (fut. ξω)

I. to strike out of, drive away from, Aesch.:—absol. to drive away, Thuc.

II. to drive out of one's senses, to amaze, astound, Od., Eur.:—often in aor2 pass., epic ἐξεπλήγην, attic ἐξεπλάγην [ᾰ]; aor1 ἐξεπλήχθην; perf. ἐκπέπληγμαι· — to be panic-struck, amazed, astonied, Il., Soph., etc.; ἐκπλαγῆναί τινι to be astonished at a thing, Hdt., so διά τι, ἐπί τινι, etc.; ἐκπλαγῆναί τινα to be struck with panic fear of . . , Soph., Thuc.

2. generally, of sudden passion, to be stricken, Aesch., etc.

B. note also the form ἐκπλήγνυμι, Thuc.
